grant.summary: Dr. Damian Young, director of the Center for Drug Discovery at Baylor College of Medicine and investigator at Texas Children's Duncan Neurological Research Institute, was awarded a $6.7 million grant from the National Institute on Aging (NIA), part of the NIH, to develop new approaches to rapidly identify potential treatments for Alzheimer's disease and related dementias.
grant.department_or_lab: Center for Drug Discovery at Baylor College of Medicine and Texas Children's Duncan Neurological Research Institute (Duncan NRI)
grant.agency: National Institute on Aging (NIA), National Institutes of Health (NIH)
grant.why_it_matters: This grant indicates significant upcoming demand for reagents, consumables, and equipment for high-throughput screening and AI-driven drug discovery for Alzheimer's disease.
